args.prompt: Read-only explorer task for LongPro. Inspect the built site files under dist_assets and identify trust, conversion, imagery, and hierarchy problems that would make the pest control site feel less credible or less premium. Focus on proof surfaces like reviews, owner/local identity, CTA consistency, imagery style, service-area pages, about/reviews/contact, and any placeholder or weak copy that undermines trust. Do not edit files. Return only concrete findings with absolute file paths and why each issue matters.

result_summary: next: 3.1 Run design-review on the live site now that the SEO/AEO fixes are live

args.note: Read-only design/trust review of dist_assets found credibility issues concentrated in AI-styled detective/cartoon imagery, weak proof surfaces (few mixed-source reviews, generic about copy, no consistent owner/base identity), CTA label inconsistency across key pages, and explicit placeholder/incomplete service-area content that makes coverage feel unfinished.

Design review found three high-impact credibility problems: detective/cartoon-style imagery leading the brand, thin proof surfaces on home/about/reviews/contact, and unfinished local-service presentation on the main service-area page. Browse text/perf checks worked, but screenshot capture returned blank images in this environment, so implementation is being driven by live HTML output plus direct asset inspection.

result_summary: next: 3.4 Re-run browser-testing-with-devtools after the design fixes for final technical verification

args.note: Repo AGENTS.md forbids non-browse browser tooling, so final technical verification was completed with the required gstack browse binary instead of Chrome DevTools MCP. Verified local preview on /, /contact/, and /service-area/cleveland/south-hills/ for console cleanliness, network/resource statuses, perf timings, metadata, labeled form controls, and mobile viewport behavior at 390x844.

result_summary: next: 4.1 Decide whether agent-native-reviewer is needed for this project based on whether we care about agent-driven f…

args.note: agent-native-reviewer is not needed for the current LongPro website scope. The site has no agent tools, system prompt, or user/agent shared workspace to audit, and the user goal here is SEO/AEO crawlability plus lead intake reliability rather than agent-driven in-app workflows. Phase 4 should focus on the public /api/contact -> PestPro intake path instead.

args.prompt: Read-only codebase audit for the LongPro -> PestPro intake path. Focus only on the /api/contact implementation, any env/secrets it depends on, and any docs or notes that describe current production status. Do not edit files. Return: 1) exact files/lines that implement intake handling, 2) required env vars/secrets and expected behavior when missing, 3) any evidence in repo docs that production is currently misconfigured or already fixed, 4) the safest next verification step. You are not alone in the codebase; do not revert or modify anything.

result_summary: next: 4.2 If PestPro intake is in scope, verify the public intake flow end to end with live config/secrets and fix any …

args.note: Read-only audit found the public intake path implemented in the compiled Worker bundle at src/index.js: the fetch handler intercepts /api/contact, validates minimal fields, requires a configured PestPro endpoint, adds optional bearer auth, and forwards JSON to PestPro. Repo docs indicate production Worker deploy/auth is already working, but PestPro intake remains misconfigured because PESTPRO_LEADS_URL and PESTPRO_INTAKE_TOKEN are not set as Worker secrets; docs cite /api/contact returning 503 until they are configured.

Verified production intake is still unconfigured: `npx wrangler secret list` returned no secrets for `longpro-scaffold`, live `POST /api/contact` returned 503 `Lead intake is not configured`, and live `GET /api/contact?status=1` now returns 503 `{ok:false,configured:false}`. Added a Worker status check plus contact-page preflight so the public form disables itself and shows a call-now fallback whenever PestPro intake is not configured. Deployed fallback in Worker version c5ff4f0e-f52c-4db4-9f7f-3756577ea1c1 and verified live `/contact/` now shows `Online Form Unavailable` with all form fields disabled. Remaining blocker for full end-to-end completion is the actual PestPro production intake URL and, if enforced, the shared intake token value.
